name: Vitthalanatha

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court references to his community-leadership.

core_thesis:
Viṭṭhalanātha completes Vallabha's unfinished commentaries and establishes the *Puṣṭi-Mārga* as a continuous institutional lineage. Vallabha's doctrine is preserved without alteration: Brahman-as-Kṛṣṇa is the sole reality (Vallabha, *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on BS 1.1.2, completed in spirit by Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation on BS 3.2 onward); the world and *jīvas* are real *aṃśas* through *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* without *māyā* (Vallabha, *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* §§*Śāstrārtha-Prakaraṇa* and *Sarva-Nirṇaya*; Viṭṭhalanātha, *Vidvan-Maṇḍana*, *jagat-prakaraṇa*); *jagat-mithyātva* is rejected (*Vidvan-Maṇḍana*, *māyā-vāda-niṣedha-prakaraṇa*); *Puṣṭi* — divine grace — is the operative soteriological principle (*Aṇu-Bhāṣya* completion on BS 3.3–3.4 and 4.1–4.4; cf. Vallabha's *Puṣṭi-Pravāha-Maryādā-Bheda*).

Three kinds of work constitute his contribution. The textual: he supplies the second half of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras* (from BS 3.2.34 onward through BS 4.4) and the unfinished portions of the *Subodhinī* on the *Bhāgavata* (notably from book 4 onward and the eleventh-book *Uddhava-Gītā* sections), completing the school's two foundational commentaries while preserving Vallabha's exegetical method and doctrinal commitments. The polemical-constructive: the *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* defends the school against rival Vedānta — principally post-Śaṅkara Advaita — at scholastic pitch, demarcating *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* from *vivartavāda* on contested points (*Vidvan-Maṇḍana*, *brahma-svarūpa-prakaraṇa* and *jīva-prakaraṇa*). The institutional: the establishment of the seven *gaddīs* (seats) under his sons, the codification of *seva* as *aṣṭa-yāma* (eightfold daily Deity-service in *śṛṅgāra* register), and the formation of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* as a stable lineage with characteristic temple practice, *kīrtana* tradition, and a developed typology distinguishing *puṣṭi*, *maryādā*, and *pravāha* souls (formalising Vallabha's *Puṣṭi-Pravāha-Maryādā-Bheda*).

The institutional achievement is what makes the doctrinal achievement liveable beyond a single generation. Without the *gaddī*-structure and the *seva*-codification, *śuddhādvaita* would have remained Vallabha's personal teaching; with them, it became a tradition with a continuous community.

engaged_works.title: Vidvan-Mandana
engaged_works.summary: The *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* ("adornment of the learned") is Viṭṭhalanātha's polemical defence of *śuddhādvaita* against rival Vedānta schools, principally post-Śaṅkara Advaita. The work proceeds topic by topic: *pramāṇa* (*Vidvan-Maṇḍana*, *pramāṇa-prakaraṇa*), the nature of Brahman (*brahma-svarūpa-prakaraṇa*), the status of the world (*jagat-prakaraṇa*), the *jīva*'s ontology (*jīva-prakaraṇa*), *bandha* and *mokṣa*, *bhakti* (*bhakti-prakaraṇa*). At each topic the *pūrvapakṣa* is staged with technical care and answered from the *śuddhādvaita* settled position. The central polemical thrust is the rejection of *māyā-vāda* and *jagat-mithyātva* (*māyā-vāda-niṣedha-prakaraṇa*). Where Advaita holds the world to be *vivarta* — non-real appearance of Brahman — Viṭṭhalanātha defends Vallabha's *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda*: the world is a real, unmodifying transformation of Brahman's own *sat-cit-ānanda* (*jagat-prakaraṇa*; cf. Vallabha, *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha*, *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa*). The work also takes care over the *jīva*'s status as *aṃśa* with *ānanda* concealed (*jīva-prakaraṇa*); the position requires careful demarcation from Viśiṣṭādvaita's *aṃśa*-doctrine, which Viṭṭhalanātha rejects on the ground that *aṃśa* needs no further qualification by *śarīra-śarīrin* once *avikṛta-pariṇāma* is admitted. It is the school's mature scholastic instrument and the standard Puṣṭimārga reference in inter-Vedāntic dispute.

engaged_works.title: Completion of Anu-Bhashya (third pada onward)
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored continuation of Vallabha's Aṇu-Bhāṣya from the third pāda.
engaged_works.summary: Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* takes up Vallabha's incomplete commentary from BS 3.2.34 (the *Tattvānusandhāna-adhikaraṇa*) and carries it through the fourth *adhyāya* (BS 4.1.1–4.4.22) to completion. The continuation maintains the terse glossing style and the doctrinal commitments of Vallabha's portion: *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda*, the rejection of *māyā-vāda*, the *aṃśa* status of *jīva* and *jagat*, *bhakti* as the operative path. The substantive engagement is with the *adhyāyas* concerned with *sādhana* (*Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on BS 3.3–3.4), the conditions and stages of *mokṣa* (BS 4.1–4.2), and the post-mortem state of the liberated (BS 4.3–4.4). Here Viṭṭhalanātha's *Puṣṭi-Mārga* commitments emerge clearly. Liberation is bestowed by Bhagavān's *anugraha*, not earned mechanically by *sādhana* (*Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on BS 3.4.1–17 and 4.1.1–13). The liberated state is participation in Kṛṣṇa's *līlā* through eternal *seva*, not absorption (*Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on BS 4.4.1–22). The continuation makes the school's Brahma-Sūtra commentary functionally available end-to-end and is the canonical Puṣṭimārga reading of the closing *adhyāyas*.

engaged_works.title: Subodhini completion
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored continuation of Vallabha's Subodhinī.
engaged_works.summary: Viṭṭhalanātha's *Subodhinī*-continuation supplies the books and passages of the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* that Vallabha did not live to gloss in his verse-by-verse commentary (notably from book 4 onward, with significant attention to books 7, 10 — beyond Vallabha's portion — and 11). The continuation preserves the father's exegetical method and *śuddhādvaita* doctrinal commitments and is doctrinally indistinguishable in tone. The difference is one of horizon: Viṭṭhalanātha is also developing the *seva*-tradition in his own right, and the continuation permits a fuller view of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* devotional theology than Vallabha's portion alone. Especially consequential are passages on the eleventh book — the *Uddhava-Gītā* (*Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata* 11.6–29) and the doctrines of *bhakti* and renunciation — and on the structures of devotion (*Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata* 10.29–33, the *Rāsa-pañcādhyāyī*) that Viṭṭhalanātha's institutional codification will then translate into temple-practice. Together with Vallabha's portion, the completed *Subodhinī* is the principal Puṣṭimārga *Bhāgavata*-commentary and the textual centre of the lineage's contemplative life.
